app.name: Another Me
system.prompt: You are a friendly assistant. Please answer the user's question.
assistant.gui.prompt:
You are a GUI agent. You are given a task and your action history, with screenshots. You need to perform the next action to complete the task.
## Output Format
```
Thought: ...
Action: ...
```
## Action Space
click(start_box="[x1, y1, x2, y2]") # Click on a specific area, coordinates are relative [0-1000]
left_double(start_box="[x1, y1, x2, y2]") # Double-click left button
right_single(start_box="[x1, y1, x2, y2]") # Single-click right button
drag(start_box="[x1, y1, x2, y2]", end_box="[x3, y3, x4, y4]") # Drag from one area to another
key_tap(keys=["key1", "key2"]) # Press and release specified keys, supports single or combined keys, e.g. ["a"] or ["ctrl", "c"]
type(content="text") # Type text. If you want to submit your input, use "\n" at the end of content.
scroll(start_box="[x1, y1, x2, y2]", direction="down or up or right or left") # Scroll in a specific direction
toggle_key(keys=["key1", "key2"], up=true/false) # Press or release specified keys, up=true means release, up=false means press
toggle_mouse(button="left/right/wheelUp", up=true/false) # Press or release mouse button, up=true means release, up=false means press
mouse_location() # Get current mouse position
scroll_relative(x=10, y=10, ms_delay=10) # Scroll mouse relative to current position
key_sleep(ms=100) # Set key interval time (milliseconds)
wait(ms=5000) # Sleep for specified time (milliseconds) and take a screenshot to check for any changes, default is 5000ms
finished(content="xxx") # Task is completed. Use escape characters \", \", and \\n in content part if needed.
## Coordinates System
- All box coordinates (start_box, end_box) must be specified as [x1, y1, x2, y2] where:
  - (x1, y1) is the top-left corner
  - (x2, y2) is the bottom-right corner
  - All values are relative coordinates from 0 to 1000 (screen is normalized)
- Be specific and accurate with coordinates by looking at the screenshot carefully
## Note
- Use Chinese in Thought part.
- In the Thought part, write a short plan and finally summarize your next action with its target element in one sentence.
- You MUST include all required parameters for each action (e.g., start_box for click)
- For text input, make sure to escape special characters properly
## User Instruction
tool.fetch.description: Fetches content from a given URL. It can simplify HTML content or return raw data.
tool.fetch.arg.url: The URL to fetch content from.
tool.fetch.arg.max_length: Maximum number of characters to return from the content (default: 10000).
tool.fetch.arg.start_index: Starting character index for fetching content (default: 0). Useful for pagination.
tool.fetch.arg.raw: If true, returns the raw content (e.g., HTML) instead of trying to simplify it (default: false).
tool.fetch.arg.ignore_robots_txt: If true, ignores the site's robots.txt rules (default: false). Use responsibly.
tool.fetch.arg.user_agent: Custom User-Agent string to use for the request. Defaults based on context (autonomous vs manual).
tool.fetch.arg.proxy_url: Optional proxy URL to use for the request (e.g., 'http://user:pass@host:port'). Uses environment proxy if unset.
tool.fetch.arg.is_manual_request: Indicates if the request is triggered manually by a user action (affects default User-Agent and robots.txt check behavior).
tool.gui.screenshot.description: Capture a screenshot of the default desktop, format like: data:image/png;base64,...
tool.gui.move_mouse.description: Move the mouse to the specified coordinates.
tool.gui.drag.description: Drag the mouse to the specified coordinates.
tool.gui.scroll.description: Scroll the mouse to the specified coordinates.
tool.gui.scroll_relative.description: Scroll the mouse to the specified coordinates relative to the current position.
tool.gui.scroll_direction.description: Scroll the mouse to the specified direction.
tool.admin.syslog.description: View system logs with filtering and time range options.
tool.admin.syslog.arg.filter: Filter logs by keyword or pattern.
tool.admin.syslog.arg.since: Show logs since this time (e.g., '1 hour ago').
tool.admin.syslog.arg.log_type: Type of log to view: kernel, system, or all.
tool.admin.sysstat.description: Monitor system resource status including CPU, memory, and disk usage.
tool.admin.sysstat.arg.resource: Resource type to monitor: cpu, memory, disk, or all.
tool.admin.procinfo.description: View process information with various detail levels.
tool.admin.procinfo.arg.pid: Process ID to get information for.
tool.admin.procinfo.arg.name: Process name to search for.
tool.admin.procinfo.arg.info_type: Type of information to display: basic, detailed, environment, files, or all.
tool.admin.procinfo.arg.count: Maximum number of processes to return when searching by name.
tool.admin.file.description: Perform file and directory operations including read, write, create, delete, and list.
tool.admin.file.arg.path: Path to the file or directory.
tool.admin.file.arg.operation: Operation to perform: read, write, create, delete, list, or exists.
tool.admin.file.arg.content: Content to write to the file.
tool.admin.file.arg.is_dir: Whether the path refers to a directory.
tool.admin.file.arg.lines: Number of lines to read (positive for first N lines, negative for last N lines).
tool.admin.file.arg.find_text: Text to find when replacing content.
tool.admin.file.arg.replace_text: Text to replace found text with.
tool.admin.archive.description: Compress and extract files in various formats including zip, tar, tar.gz, and more.
tool.admin.archive.arg.operation: Operation to perform: compress or extract.
tool.admin.archive.arg.source_path: Path to the source file or directory.
tool.admin.archive.arg.target_path: Path to the target file or directory.
tool.admin.archive.arg.format: Archive format to use: zip, tar, targz, tarbz2, or tarxz.
tool.admin.archive.arg.source_paths: Multiple source paths to compress (when compressing multiple files/directories).
tool.admin.archive.arg.verbose: Whether to display detailed information during operation.
